layman_headline: Crypto market is in 'Fear' mode — patience beats impulse right now.
layman_explanation: The overall crypto market is fearful, with Bitcoin dominant at 56.7% and most altcoins in bearish chart structures. While some coins have bounced in the last 24 hours, the underlying trends are still pointing down for most alternatives to Bitcoin. None of the swing candidates present a clean, high-conviction setup with both technical and news catalysts aligned. Ethereum is the strongest candidate but needs to pull back to better support before it becomes a quality entry.
layman_action: Stay on the sidelines for now. Set price alerts for Ethereum around $1,810 and wait for a cleaner entry. Don't chase the small bounce — let the trade come to you.
expert_reasoning: ETH's RSI at 39.42 is approaching oversold territory (reliable signal per our track record — 80% win rate). However, the chart bias is -0.70 (bearish), price is below all major MAs, and the 7-day +7% move means we're chasing rather than buying the dip. BTC is hitting a $65K wall per CoinTelegraph reporting, and with BTC dominance at 56.7%, alts face headwinds. The Fear & Greed index at 29 is improving from 25.9 weekly average, which is constructive but not yet at the extreme fear (sub-20) levels that produce the highest-conviction contrarian entries. Best strategy is to wait for ETH to retest $1,800-$1,810 support for a 1.4x+ risk/reward entry.
risk_factors: BTC failing to break $65K resistance could trigger another leg down across all alts
High BTC dominance (56.7%) signals capital rotation away from alts — ETH underperformance may continue
No MACD cross or volume breakout confirmation on any candidate — all setups lack technical conviction
watch_list: BTC price action at $65K resistance — a decisive break above opens $72K target and lifts all alts
ETH retest of $1,800 support — would create a much better entry with clear stop-loss at $1,750
Fear & Greed index trajectory — a drop below 20 would signal extreme fear and higher-conviction contrarian entry
market_context: Bitcoin is testing $65K resistance amid institutional tech sell-offs, while the Fear & Greed index sits at 29 (Fear) but is improving. BTC dominance at 56.7% continues to pressure altcoins, making this a difficult environment for alt swing trades.
context_banner: Market in Fear with improving trend — best setups emerge when patience meets a confirmed support retest.

key_signals.description: ETH RSI at 39.42 is approaching oversold but not yet at the sub-30 extreme that historically produces our best entries (80% win rate on RSI signals).
BTC hitting $65K wall as stocks battle record institutional tech sell-off — key resistance level that determines whether alts get a tailwind or headwind.
Fear index at 29 improving from 25.9 weekly average — constructive but not yet at extreme fear levels that produce highest-conviction contrarian entries.
$2.5B options bet targeting BTC $72K by August on Fed rally expectations — if realized, would lift ETH significantly given its higher beta.
ETH derivatives reset reported — overleveraged positions have been flushed, creating cleaner positioning for the next directional move.
